payload.answer: A conflict was detected between two concurrent memory candidates: “Sam works at Goldman” and “Sam joined Point72.” Safe actions available are: approve_supersession, keep_both, reject_new, or edit. Choose approve_supersession only if the Point72 statement should replace the Goldman employment statement. Choose keep_both if both statements should be retained, for example if they refer to different time periods or roles. Choose reject_new if the new Point72 statement should not be saved. Choose edit if the wording needs clarification before saving, such as adding dates, role details, or distinguishing Goldman from Point72.
citations: Concurrent writes - Sam works at Goldman Sam joined Point72

entity_resolution.reason: Both statements refer to Sam, but the input does not clarify whether joining Point72 supersedes working at Goldman, whether both are true, or whether one statement is incorrect.

details: No durable memory was committed because the concurrent statements may conflict.
The ambiguity was preserved rather than assuming supersession or overwriting an existing fact.
